description: Young Adult requires a protagonist in active identity flux, a peer social world as primary relational fabric, and authority figures who are simultaneously shelter and cage — every choice must feel world-defining from inside the protagonist's perspective.

character_constraints.description: A young person in visible, active identity flux — discovering who they are through choices and consequences.
Age-mates who constitute the protagonist's primary social world — friends, rivals, love interests.
An adult who is simultaneously shelter and cage — parent, teacher, mentor whose protection also constrains.

relationship_constraints.description: Bonds of friendship and allegiance that are tested and redefined through the story.
Tension between the protagonist's emerging identity and the authority figure's expectations or rules.

place_constraints.description: School, academy, faction, or community where peer dynamics play out under institutional rules.
A space for identity exploration away from social and authority pressure — bedroom, hideout, secret meeting place.

object_constraints.description: An object adopted or rejected as part of identity formation — uniform, token, accessory, or inherited item.
An object that represents a peer bond — shared secret, gift, or symbol of belonging.

element_rules.description: The protagonist must visibly change identity across the story — who they are at the end is different from who they were at the start.
element_rules.testable_condition: The protagonist's self-description or defining trait in the final quarter differs from the first quarter.

element_rules.description: Every major choice must feel world-defining from inside the protagonist's perspective — no adult relativizing or diminishing the stakes.
element_rules.testable_condition: At least 2 decision transitions are framed as irreversible from the protagonist's point of view.
